name: Angle Explorer
icon: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64" fill="none"><rect width="64" height="64" rx="16" fill="#0f172a"/><line x1="32" y1="32" x2="56" y2="32" stroke="#38bdf8" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="32" y1="32" x2="14" y2="14" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round"/><path d="M 48 32 A 16 16 0 0 0 37.3 20.7" stroke="#facc15" stroke-width="2" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="42" y="28" font-size="7" fill="#facc15" font-family="monospace" font-weight="bold">135°</text><circle cx="32" cy="32" r="3" fill="#38bdf8"/></svg>
description:
Angle Explorer is an interactive arcade-style maths game for learning all major angle types through a draggable Swiss-Army-knife ray mechanic.

WHAT IT DOES:
A movable ray sweeps from a fixed baseline, like opening a knife blade. As it rotates the live angle value updates. Key positions (90°, 180°, 360°) snap and lock. Multiple blades can be added to explore multi-ray fan diagrams.

WHAT IT TEACHES:
- Level 1: Recognising zero, acute, right, obtuse, straight, reflex and full-turn angles by sight
- Level 2: Complementary angles — two angles summing to 90°
- Level 3: Supplementary angles — two angles on a straight line summing to 180°
- Level 4: Adjacent angle fans — 3+ rays from a vertex, add known parts, find the residual
- Level 5: Vertical (opposite) angles — equal angles at a crossing
- Level 6: Reflex and full-turn angles — past 180° back to 360°

INTERACTION MODEL:
Each level has White Eggs (free drag exploration with live angle readout) and Golden Eggs (static diagrams, no drag aid — type the answer). A Predict-then-Drag bridge question appears between modes.

PEDAGOGICAL NOTES:
- Targets the specific error pattern of adding known angle parts before subtracting from a total
- Snap-and-lock at 90° and 180° builds physical intuition for the key totals
- Vertical angle cheat-code moment rewards pattern recognition

TECH: React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, SVG.
